turns.raw:
Nakalipas na ang oras.
But this is "Time has passed." The best way to say "I am late" in Tagalog is "Tanggap na ako" or "Nakalipas na ang oras, kaya late na ako." But a common short way is "Late na ako." But to be natural, "Tanggap na ako." But if you want a direct translation, "Ako ay huli." But "huli" means "last" or "late (as in due date)."

But the most common is "Late na ako." But if you want to avoid "ako" (I), you can't, because it's a sentence about the speaker.

But you just want the translation, so:

Late na ako.
But you want no 'assistant', so just:

Late na ako.
